description: You can use Spotify's Web API to discover music and podcasts, manage your Spotify library, control audio playback

tools.name: albums_get-an
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single album. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the AlbumObject.

tools.name: albums_get-multiple
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for multiple albums identified by their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of AlbumObject.

tools.name: albums_tracks_get-an-albums
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about an album’s tracks. Optional parameters can be used to limit the number of tracks returned. Required: id. Optional: market, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSimplifiedTrackObject.

tools.name: artists_get-an
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single artist identified by their unique Spotify ID. Required: id. Returns the ArtistObject.

tools.name: artists_get-multiple
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for several artists based on their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Returns array of ArtistObject.

tools.name: artists_albums_get-an-artists
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about an artist's albums. Required: id. Optional: include_groups, market, limit (default: 5) (plus 1 more). Returns the PagingArtistDiscographyAlbumObject.

tools.name: artists_related-artists_get-an-artists
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about artists similar to a given artist. Similarity is based on analysis of the Spotify community's listening history. Required: id. Returns array of ArtistObject.

tools.name: artists_top-tracks_get-an-artists
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about an artist's top tracks by country. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns array of TrackObject.

tools.name: audio-analysis_get
tools.description: Get a low-level audio analysis for a track in the Spotify catalog. The audio analysis describes the track’s structure and musical content, including rhythm, pitch, and timbre. Required: id. Returns the AudioAnalysisObject.

tools.name: audio-features_get
tools.description: Get audio feature information for a single track identified by its unique Spotify ID. Required: id. Returns the AudioFeaturesObject.

tools.name: audio-features_get-several
tools.description: Get audio features for multiple tracks based on their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Returns array of AudioFeaturesObject.

tools.name: audiobooks_get-an
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single audiobook. Audiobooks are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the AudiobookObject.

tools.name: audiobooks_get-multiple
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for several audiobooks identified by their Spotify IDs. Audiobooks are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of AudiobookObject.

tools.name: audiobooks_chapters_get-audiobook
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about an audiobook's chapters. Audiobooks are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: id. Optional: market, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSimplifiedChapterObject.

tools.name: browse_get-a-categories-playlists
tools.description: Get a list of Spotify playlists tagged with a particular category. Required: category_id. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingFeaturedPlaylistObject.

tools.name: browse_get-a-category
tools.description: Get a single category used to tag items in Spotify (on, for example, the Spotify player’s “Browse” tab). Required: category_id. Optional: locale. Returns array of ImageObject.

tools.name: browse_get-categories
tools.description: Get a list of categories used to tag items in Spotify (on, for example, the Spotify player’s “Browse” tab). Optional: locale, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the BrowseGetCategoriesResponse.

tools.name: browse_get-featured-playlists
tools.description: Get a list of Spotify featured playlists (shown, for example, on a Spotify player's 'Browse' tab). Optional: locale, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingFeaturedPlaylistObject.

tools.name: browse_get-new-releases
tools.description: Get a list of new album releases featured in Spotify (shown, for example, on a Spotify player’s “Browse” tab). Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the BrowseGetNewReleasesResponse.

tools.name: chapters_get-a
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single audiobook chapter. Chapters are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the ChapterObject.

tools.name: chapters_get-several
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for several audiobook chapters identified by their Spotify IDs. Chapters are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of ChapterObject.

tools.name: episodes_get-an
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single episode identified by its unique Spotify ID. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the EpisodeObject.

tools.name: episodes_get-multiple
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for several episodes based on their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of EpisodeObject.

tools.name: markets_get-available
tools.description: Get the list of markets where Spotify is available. Returns array of string.

tools.name: me_add-to-queue
tools.description: Add an item to be played next in the user's current playback queue. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: uri. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_check-current-user-follows
tools.description: Check to see if the current user is following one or more artists or other Spotify users. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: type, ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-library-contains
tools.description: Check if one or more items are already saved in the current user's library. Accepts Spotify URIs for tracks, albums, episodes, shows, audiobooks, artists, users, and playlists. Required: uris. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-users-saved-albums
tools.description: Check if one or more albums is already saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-users-saved-audiobooks
tools.description: Check if one or more audiobooks are already saved in the current Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-users-saved-episodes
tools.description: Check if one or more episodes is already saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Episodes' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-users-saved-shows
tools.description: Check if one or more shows is already saved in the current Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_check-users-saved-tracks
tools.description: Check if one or more tracks is already saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: me_create-playlist
tools.description: Create a playlist for the current Spotify user. (The playlist will be empty until you [add tracks](/documentation/web-api/reference/add-tracks-to-playlist).) Each user is generally limited to a maximum of 11000 playlists. Required: name. Optional: collaborative, description, public. Returns array of ImageObject.

params.name: collaborative
params.description: Defaults to `false`. If `true` the playlist will be collaborative. _**Note**: to create a collaborative playlist you must also set `public` to `false`. To create collaborative playlists you must have granted `playlist-modify-private` and `playlist-modify-public` [scopes](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes)._

params.name: description
params.description: value for playlist description as displayed in Spotify Clients and in the Web API.

params.name: name
params.description: The name for the new playlist, for example `'Your Coolest Playlist'`. This name does not need to be unique; a user may have several playlists with the same name.

params.name: public
params.description: Defaults to `true`. The playlist's public/private status (if it should be added to the user's profile or not): `true` the playlist will be public, `false` the playlist will be private. To be able to create private playlists, the user must have granted the `playlist-modify-private` [scope](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes). For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

tools.name: me_follow-artists-users
tools.description: Add the current user as a follower of one or more artists or other Spotify users. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: type, ids, ids-2.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the artist or user [Spotify IDs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids). For example: `{ids:['74ASZWbe4lXaubB36ztrGX', '08td7MxkoHQkXnWAYD8d6Q']}`. A maximum of 50 IDs can be sent in one request. _**Note**: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: me_get-a-list-of-current-users-playlists
tools.description: Get a list of the playlists owned or followed by the current Spotify user. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingPlaylistObject.

tools.name: me_get-a-users-available-devices
tools.description: Get information about a user’s available Spotify Connect devices. Some device models are not supported and will not be listed in the API response. Returns array of DeviceObject.

tools.name: me_get-current-users-profile
tools.description: Get detailed profile information about the current user (including the current user's username). Returns array of ImageObject.

tools.name: me_get-followed
tools.description: Get the current user's followed artists. Required: type. Optional: after, limit (default: 20). Returns the MeGetFollowedResponse.

tools.name: me_get-information-about-the-users-current-playback
tools.description: Get information about the user’s current playback state, including track or episode, progress, and active device. Optional: market, additional_types. Returns the CurrentlyPlayingContextObject.

tools.name: me_get-queue
tools.description: Get the list of objects that make up the user's queue. Returns array of MeGetQueueItem.

tools.name: me_get-recently-played
tools.description: Get tracks from the current user's recently played tracks. _**Note**: Currently doesn't support podcast episodes._. Optional: limit (default: 20), after, before. Returns the CursorPagingPlayHistoryObject.

tools.name: me_get-the-users-currently-playing-track
tools.description: Get the object currently being played on the user's Spotify account. Optional: market, additional_types. Returns the CurrentlyPlayingObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-saved-albums
tools.description: Get a list of the albums saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Music' library. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0), market. Returns the PagingSavedAlbumObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-saved-audiobooks
tools.description: Get a list of the audiobooks saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Music' library. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSavedAudiobookObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-saved-episodes
tools.description: Get a list of the episodes saved in the current Spotify user's library. Optional: market, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSavedEpisodeObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-saved-shows
tools.description: Get a list of shows saved in the current Spotify user's library. Optional parameters can be used to limit the number of shows returned. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSavedShowObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-saved-tracks
tools.description: Get a list of the songs saved in the current Spotify user's 'Your Music' library. Optional: market, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSavedTrackObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-top-artists
tools.description: Get the current user's top artists based on calculated affinity. Optional: time_range (default: medium_term), limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingArtistObject.

tools.name: me_get-users-top-tracks
tools.description: Get the current user's top tracks based on calculated affinity. Optional: time_range (default: medium_term), limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingTrackObject.

tools.name: me_pause-a-users-playback
tools.description: Pause playback on the user's account. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_remove-albums-user
tools.description: Remove one or more albums from the current user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: ids-2. Destructive.

tools.name: me_remove-audiobooks-user
tools.description: Remove one or more audiobooks from the Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Destructive.

tools.name: me_remove-episodes-user
tools.description: Remove one or more episodes from the current user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: ids-2. Destructive.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify IDs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids). A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. _**Note**: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: me_remove-library-items
tools.description: Remove one or more items from the current user's library. Accepts Spotify URIs for tracks, albums, episodes, shows, audiobooks, users, and playlists. Required: uris. Destructive.

tools.name: me_remove-shows-user
tools.description: Delete one or more shows from current Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: market, ids-2. Destructive.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify IDs](https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api/#spotify-uris-and-ids). A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. *Note: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored.*

tools.name: me_remove-tracks-user
tools.description: Remove one or more tracks from the current user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: ids-2. Destructive.

tools.name: me_save-albums-user
tools.description: Save one or more albums to the current user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: ids-2.

tools.name: me_save-audiobooks-user
tools.description: Save one or more audiobooks to the current Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: ids.

tools.name: me_save-episodes-user
tools.description: Save one or more episodes to the current user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: ids, ids-2.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify IDs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids). A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. _**Note**: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: me_save-library-items
tools.description: Add one or more items to the current user's library. Accepts Spotify URIs for tracks, albums, episodes, shows, audiobooks, users, and playlists. Required: uris.

tools.name: me_save-shows-user
tools.description: Save one or more shows to current Spotify user's library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: ids-2.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify IDs](https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api/#spotify-uris-and-ids). A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. *Note: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored.*

tools.name: me_save-tracks-user
tools.description: Save one or more tracks to the current user's 'Your Music' library. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: ids. Optional: timestamped_ids.

params.name: ids
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify IDs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids). For example: `['4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh', '1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M']` A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. _**Note**: if the `timestamped_ids` is present in the body, any IDs listed in the query parameters (deprecated) or the `ids` field in the body will be ignored._

params.name: timestamped_ids
params.description: A JSON array of objects containing track IDs with their corresponding timestamps. Each object must include a track ID and an `added_at` timestamp. This allows you to specify when tracks were added to maintain a specific chronological order in the user's library. A maximum of 50 items can be specified in one request. _**Note**: if the `timestamped_ids` is present in the body, any IDs listed in the query parameters (deprecated) or the `ids` field in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: me_seek-to-position-in-currently-playing-track
tools.description: Seeks to the given position in the user’s currently playing track. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: position_ms. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_set-repeat-mode-on-users-playback
tools.description: Set the repeat mode for the user's playback. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: state. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_set-volume-for-users-playback
tools.description: Set the volume for the user’s current playback device. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: volume_percent. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_skip-users-playback-to-next-track
tools.description: Skips to next track in the user’s queue. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_skip-users-playback-to-previous-track
tools.description: Skips to previous track in the user’s queue. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_start-a-users-playback
tools.description: Start a new context or resume current playback on the user's active device. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Optional: device_id, context_uri, offset (plus 2 more).

params.name: context_uri
params.description: Optional. Spotify URI of the context to play. Valid contexts are albums, artists & playlists. `{context_uri:'spotify:album:1Je1IMUlBXcx1Fz0WE7oPT'}`

params.name: offset
params.description: Optional. Indicates from where in the context playback should start. Only available when context_uri corresponds to an album or playlist object 'position' is zero based and can’t be negative. Example: `'offset': {'position': 5}` 'uri' is a string representing the uri of the item to start at. Example: `'offset': {'uri': 'spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M'}`

params.name: position_ms
params.description: Indicates from what position to start playback. Must be a positive number. Passing in a position that is greater than the length of the track will cause the player to start playing the next song.

params.name: uris
params.description: Optional. A JSON array of the Spotify track URIs to play. For example: `{'uris': ['spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh', 'spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M']}`

tools.name: me_toggle-shuffle-for-users-playback
tools.description: Toggle shuffle on or off for user’s playback. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: state. Optional: device_id.

tools.name: me_transfer-a-users-playback
tools.description: Transfer playback to a new device and optionally begin playback. This API only works for users who have Spotify Premium. The order of execution is not guaranteed when you use this API with other Player API endpoints. Required: device_ids. Optional: play.

params.name: device_ids
params.description: A JSON array containing the ID of the device on which playback should be started/transferred. For example:`{device_ids:['74ASZWbe4lXaubB36ztrGX']}` _**Note**: Although an array is accepted, only a single device_id is currently supported. Supplying more than one will return `400 Bad Request`_

params.name: play
params.description: **true**: ensure playback happens on new device. **false** or not provided: keep the current playback state.

tools.name: me_unfollow-artists-users
tools.description: Remove the current user as a follower of one or more artists or other Spotify users. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: type, ids. Optional: ids-2. Destructive.

params.name: ids-2
params.description: A JSON array of the artist or user [Spotify IDs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids). For example: `{ids:['74ASZWbe4lXaubB36ztrGX', '08td7MxkoHQkXnWAYD8d6Q']}`. A maximum of 50 IDs can be sent in one request. _**Note**: if the `ids` parameter is present in the query string, any IDs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: playlists_change-details
tools.description: Change a playlist's name and public/private state. (The user must, of course, own the playlist.). Required: playlist_id. Optional: collaborative, description, name (plus 1 more).

params.name: collaborative
params.description: If `true`, the playlist will become collaborative and other users will be able to modify the playlist in their Spotify client. _**Note**: You can only set `collaborative` to `true` on non-public playlists._

params.name: description
params.description: Value for playlist description as displayed in Spotify Clients and in the Web API.

params.name: name
params.description: The new name for the playlist, for example `'My New Playlist Title'`

params.name: public
params.description: The playlist's public/private status (if it should be added to the user's profile or not): `true` the playlist will be public, `false` the playlist will be private, `null` the playlist status is not relevant. For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

tools.name: playlists_get
tools.description: Get a playlist owned by a Spotify user. Required: playlist_id. Optional: market, fields, additional_types. Returns array of ImageObject.

tools.name: playlists_followers_check-if-user-follows-playlist
tools.description: Check to see if the current user is following a specified playlist. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Check User's Saved Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/check-library-contains) instead. Required: playlist_id. Optional: ids. Returns array of bool.

tools.name: playlists_followers_follow-playlist
tools.description: Add the current user as a follower of a playlist. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Save Items to Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/save-library-items) instead. Required: playlist_id. Optional: public.

params.name: public
params.description: Defaults to `true`. If `true` the playlist will be included in user's public playlists (added to profile), if `false` it will remain private. For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

tools.name: playlists_followers_unfollow-playlist
tools.description: Remove the current user as a follower of a playlist. **Note:** This endpoint is deprecated. Use [Remove Items from Library](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-library-items) instead. Required: playlist_id. Destructive.

tools.name: playlists_images_get-playlist-cover
tools.description: Get the current image associated with a specific playlist. Required: playlist_id. Returns array of ImageObject.

tools.name: playlists_images_upload-custom-playlist-cover
tools.description: Replace the image used to represent a specific playlist. Required: playlist_id.

tools.name: playlists_items_add-to-playlist
tools.description: Add one or more items to a user's playlist. Required: playlist_id. Optional: position, uris, position-2 (plus 1 more). Returns the new ItemsAddToPlaylistResponse.

params.name: position-2
params.description: The position to insert the items, a zero-based index. For example, to insert the items in the first position: `position=0` ; to insert the items in the third position: `position=2`. If omitted, the items will be appended to the playlist. Items are added in the order they appear in the uris array. For example: `{'uris': ['spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh','spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M'], 'position': 3}`

params.name: uris-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) to add. For example: `{'uris': ['spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh','spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M', 'spotify:episode:512ojhOuo1ktJprKbVcKyQ']}` A maximum of 100 items can be added in one request. _**Note**: if the `uris` parameter is present in the query string, any URIs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: playlists_items_get-playlists
tools.description: Get full details of the items of a playlist owned by a Spotify user. **Note**: This endpoint is only accessible for playlists owned by the current user or playlists the user is a collaborator of. A `403 Forbidden` status code will be returned if the user is neither the owner nor a collaborator of the playlist. Required: playlist_id. Optional: market, fields, limit (default: 20) (plus 2 more). Returns the PagingPlaylistTrackObject.

tools.name: playlists_items_remove-playlist
tools.description: Remove one or more items from a user's playlist. Required: playlist_id, items. Optional: snapshot_id. Returns the ItemsRemovePlaylistResponse. Destructive.

params.name: items
params.description: An array of objects containing [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) of the tracks or episodes to remove. For example: `{ 'items': [{ 'uri': 'spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh' },{ 'uri': 'spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M' }] }`. A maximum of 100 objects can be sent at once.

params.name: snapshot_id
params.description: The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes. The API will validate that the specified items exist and in the specified positions and make the changes, even if more recent changes have been made to the playlist.

tools.name: playlists_items_reorder-or-replace-playlists
tools.description: Either reorder or replace items in a playlist depending on the request's parameters. To reorder items, include `range_start`, `insert_before`, `range_length` and `snapshot_id` in the request's body. To replace items, include `uris` as either a query parameter or in the request's body. Replacing items in a playlist will overwrite its existing items. This operation can be used for replacing or clearing items in a playlist. **Note**: Replace and reorder are mutually exclusive operations which share the same endpoint, but have different parameters. These operations can't be applied together in a single request. Required: playlist_id. Optional: uris, insert_before, range_length (plus 3 more). Returns the updated ItemsReorderOrReplacePlaylistsResponse.

params.name: insert_before
params.description: The position where the items should be inserted. To reorder the items to the end of the playlist, simply set _insert_before_ to the position after the last item. Examples: To reorder the first item to the last position in a playlist with 10 items, set _range_start_ to 0, and _insert_before_ to 10. To reorder the last item in a playlist with 10 items to the start of the playlist, set _range_start_ to 9, and _insert_before_ to 0.

params.name: range_length
params.description: The amount of items to be reordered. Defaults to 1 if not set. The range of items to be reordered begins from the _range_start_ position, and includes the _range_length_ subsequent items. Example: To move the items at index 9-10 to the start of the playlist, _range_start_ is set to 9, and _range_length_ is set to 2.

params.name: range_start
params.description: The position of the first item to be reordered.

params.name: snapshot_id
params.description: The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes.

tools.name: playlists_tracks_add-to-playlist
tools.description: **Deprecated:** Use [Add Items to Playlist](/documentation/web-api/reference/add-items-to-playlist) instead. Add one or more items to a user's playlist. Required: playlist_id. Optional: position, uris, position-2 (plus 1 more). Returns the new TracksAddToPlaylistResponse.

params.name: position-2
params.description: The position to insert the items, a zero-based index. For example, to insert the items in the first position: `position=0` ; to insert the items in the third position: `position=2`. If omitted, the items will be appended to the playlist. Items are added in the order they appear in the uris array. For example: `{'uris': ['spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh','spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M'], 'position': 3}`

params.name: uris-2
params.description: A JSON array of the [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) to add. For example: `{'uris': ['spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh','spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M', 'spotify:episode:512ojhOuo1ktJprKbVcKyQ']}` A maximum of 100 items can be added in one request. _**Note**: if the `uris` parameter is present in the query string, any URIs listed here in the body will be ignored._

tools.name: playlists_tracks_get-playlists
tools.description: **Deprecated:** Use [Get Playlist Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/get-playlists-items) instead. Get full details of the items of a playlist owned by a Spotify user. Required: playlist_id. Optional: market, fields, limit (default: 20) (plus 2 more). Returns the PagingPlaylistTrackObject.

tools.name: playlists_tracks_remove-playlist
tools.description: **Deprecated:** Use [Remove Playlist Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/remove-items-playlist) instead. Remove one or more items from a user's playlist. Required: playlist_id, tracks. Optional: snapshot_id. Returns the TracksRemovePlaylistResponse. Destructive.

params.name: snapshot_id
params.description: The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes. The API will validate that the specified items exist and in the specified positions and make the changes, even if more recent changes have been made to the playlist.

params.name: tracks
params.description: An array of objects containing [Spotify URIs](/documentation/web-api/concepts/spotify-uris-ids) of the tracks or episodes to remove. For example: `{ 'tracks': [{ 'uri': 'spotify:track:4iV5W9uYEdYUVa79Axb7Rh' },{ 'uri': 'spotify:track:1301WleyT98MSxVHPZCA6M' }] }`. A maximum of 100 objects can be sent at once.

tools.name: playlists_tracks_reorder-or-replace-playlists
tools.description: **Deprecated:** Use [Update Playlist Items](/documentation/web-api/reference/reorder-or-replace-playlists-items) instead. Either reorder or replace items in a playlist depending on the request's parameters. To reorder items, include `range_start`, `insert_before`, `range_length` and `snapshot_id` in the request's body. To replace items, include `uris` as either a query parameter or in the request's body. Replacing items in a playlist will overwrite its existing items. This operation can be used for replacing or clearing items in a playlist. **Note**: Replace and reorder are mutually exclusive operations which share the same endpoint, but have different parameters. These operations can't be applied together in a single request. Required: playlist_id. Optional: uris, insert_before, range_length (plus 3 more). Returns the updated TracksReorderOrReplacePlaylistsResponse.

params.name: insert_before
params.description: The position where the items should be inserted. To reorder the items to the end of the playlist, simply set _insert_before_ to the position after the last item. Examples: To reorder the first item to the last position in a playlist with 10 items, set _range_start_ to 0, and _insert_before_ to 10. To reorder the last item in a playlist with 10 items to the start of the playlist, set _range_start_ to 9, and _insert_before_ to 0.

params.name: range_length
params.description: The amount of items to be reordered. Defaults to 1 if not set. The range of items to be reordered begins from the _range_start_ position, and includes the _range_length_ subsequent items. Example: To move the items at index 9-10 to the start of the playlist, _range_start_ is set to 9, and _range_length_ is set to 2.

params.name: range_start
params.description: The position of the first item to be reordered.

params.name: snapshot_id
params.description: The playlist's snapshot ID against which you want to make the changes.

tools.name: recommendations_get
tools.description: Recommendations are generated based on the available information for a given seed entity and matched against similar artists and tracks. If there is sufficient information about the provided seeds, a list of tracks will be returned together with pool size details. For artists and tracks that are very new or obscure there might not be enough data to generate a list of tracks. Optional: limit (default: 20), market, seed_artists (plus 44 more). Returns the RecommendationsObject.

tools.name: recommendations_get-genres
tools.description: Retrieve a list of available genres seed parameter values for [recommendations](/documentation/web-api/reference/get-recommendations). Returns array of string.

tools.name: shows_get-a
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single show identified by its unique Spotify ID. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the ShowObject.

tools.name: shows_get-multiple
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for several shows based on their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of SimplifiedShowObject.

tools.name: shows_episodes_get-a-shows
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about an show’s episodes. Optional parameters can be used to limit the number of episodes returned. Required: id. Optional: market, limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingSimplifiedEpisodeObject.

tools.name: spotify_web_search_search
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information about albums, artists, playlists, tracks, shows, episodes or audiobooks that match a keyword string. Audiobooks are only available within the US, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and Australia markets. Required: q, type. Optional: market, limit (default: 5), offset (default: 0) (plus 1 more). Returns the SpotifyWebSearchSearchResponse.

tools.name: tracks_get
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for a single track identified by its unique Spotify ID. Required: id. Optional: market. Returns the TrackObject.

tools.name: tracks_get-several
tools.description: Get Spotify catalog information for multiple tracks based on their Spotify IDs. Required: ids. Optional: market. Returns array of TrackObject.

tools.name: users_get-profile
tools.description: Get public profile information about a Spotify user. Required: user_id. Returns array of ImageObject.

tools.name: users_playlists_create-for-user
tools.description: **Deprecated**: Use [Create Playlist](/documentation/web-api/reference/create-playlist) instead. Create a playlist for a Spotify user. (The playlist will be empty until you [add tracks](/documentation/web-api/reference/add-tracks-to-playlist).) Each user is generally limited to a maximum of 11000 playlists. Required: user_id, name. Optional: collaborative, description, public. Returns array of ImageObject.

params.name: collaborative
params.description: Defaults to `false`. If `true` the playlist will be collaborative. _**Note**: to create a collaborative playlist you must also set `public` to `false`. To create collaborative playlists you must have granted `playlist-modify-private` and `playlist-modify-public` [scopes](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes)._

params.name: description
params.description: value for playlist description as displayed in Spotify Clients and in the Web API.

params.name: name
params.description: The name for the new playlist, for example `'Your Coolest Playlist'`. This name does not need to be unique; a user may have several playlists with the same name.

params.name: public
params.description: Defaults to `true`. The playlist's public/private status (if it should be added to the user's profile or not): `true` the playlist will be public, `false` the playlist will be private. To be able to create private playlists, the user must have granted the `playlist-modify-private` [scope](/documentation/web-api/concepts/scopes/#list-of-scopes). For more about public/private status, see [Working with Playlists](/documentation/web-api/concepts/playlists)

tools.name: users_playlists_get-list-users
tools.description: Get a list of the playlists owned or followed by a Spotify user. Required: user_id. Optional: limit (default: 20), offset (default: 0). Returns the PagingPlaylistObject.
